examples: Cyclohexene was synthesized from cyclohexanol using sodium bisulfate as catalyst.
Results: the yield was over 98%, and most of the sodium bisulfate was in the solid form.
Sodium bisulfate can be used as a catalyst for esterification instead of sulphuric acid.
Supposedly the science started in the 1850s when, in the lab of an English scientist, a dog that had been fed quinine bisulfate for an upset stomach urinated on a tray.
